auth.label: Device Key
auth.description: Bark device key used as the device_key field for push requests. Open the Bark iOS app and copy the push URL shown in the tutorial, then paste either the key or full URL: https://bark.day.app/#/tutorial.

extraFields.label: Server Base URL
extraFields.description: Optional Bark server base URL. Leave empty to use https://api.day.app, or enter your self-hosted Bark server URL from the Bark Server API docs: https://github.com/Finb/bark-server/blob/master/docs/API_V2.md.

actions.name: get_server_info
actions.description: Fetch raw server information from the connected Bark server.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for fetching Bark server information.

raw.description: The raw payload returned by the Bark server info endpoint.

outputSchema.description: The wrapped Bark server information response.

actions.name: send_batch_notifications
actions.description: Send the same notification to multiple explicit Bark device keys through the REST push endpoint.

items.description: One Bark device key. This value is sensitive.

device_keys.description: The Bark device keys that should receive this notification.

title.description: Optional notification title shown above the body.

body.description: Notification body text to send to the Bark device.

level.description: Optional iOS interruption level for the notification.

volume.description: Optional ringtone volume for critical alert notifications.

badge.description: Optional badge number displayed next to the Bark app icon.

call.description: Whether the ringtone should continue to play for 30 seconds.

autoCopy.description: Whether Bark should automatically copy the notification content.

copy.description: Optional text value Bark should copy.

sound.description: Optional Bark sound name or custom ringtone name.

icon.description: Optional icon URL shown with the notification on supported iOS versions.

isArchive.description: Whether Bark should archive the notification in the app.

url.description: Optional URL opened when the user taps the notification.

action.description: Optional tap behavior for the notification.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for sending one Bark notification to multiple device keys.

code.description: The Bark response code. Successful requests usually return 200.

timestamp.description: The Bark server timestamp for the response.

outputSchema.description: The normalized Bark response returned after a push request.

actions.name: send_encrypted_notification
actions.description: Send a pre-encrypted Bark notification ciphertext to the connected Bark device.

ciphertext.description: The already-encrypted Bark ciphertext payload to forward.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for sending one encrypted Bark notification.

code.description: The Bark response code. Successful requests usually return 200.

timestamp.description: The Bark server timestamp for the response.

outputSchema.description: The normalized Bark response returned after a push request.

actions.name: send_notification
actions.description: Send a notification to the connected Bark device through the REST push endpoint.

title.description: Optional notification title shown above the body.

body.description: Notification body text to send to the Bark device.

level.description: Optional iOS interruption level for the notification.

volume.description: Optional ringtone volume for critical alert notifications.

badge.description: Optional badge number displayed next to the Bark app icon.

call.description: Whether the ringtone should continue to play for 30 seconds.

autoCopy.description: Whether Bark should automatically copy the notification content.

copy.description: Optional text value Bark should copy.

sound.description: Optional Bark sound name or custom ringtone name.

icon.description: Optional icon URL shown with the notification on supported iOS versions.

isArchive.description: Whether Bark should archive the notification in the app.

url.description: Optional URL opened when the user taps the notification.

action.description: Optional tap behavior for the notification.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for sending one Bark notification.

code.description: The Bark response code. Successful requests usually return 200.

timestamp.description: The Bark server timestamp for the response.

outputSchema.description: The normalized Bark response returned after a push request.
